---
title: "Statistical Analysis in R"
teaching: 80
exercises: 40
---

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: questions

- How do I run t-tests, ANOVA, correlations, and regression in R?
- How does R output compare to SPSS output tables?
- How do I extract and report results?

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: objectives

- Run independent and paired samples t-tests
- Conduct one-way ANOVA with post-hoc tests
- Calculate correlations and run simple linear regression
- Interpret R output by mapping it to familiar SPSS output tables

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

## From SPSS dialogs to R functions

TODO: For each test, show a screenshot of the SPSS dialog alongside R code:

### T-tests

- Analyze > Compare Means > Independent-Samples T Test = `t.test(y ~ group, data = df)`
- Analyze > Compare Means > Paired-Samples T Test = `t.test(x, y, paired = TRUE)`

### ANOVA

- Analyze > Compare Means > One-Way ANOVA = `aov(y ~ group, data = df)` + `summary()`
- Post-hoc: `TukeyHSD()`

### Correlation

- Analyze > Correlate > Bivariate = `cor.test(df$x, df$y)`

### Regression

- Analyze > Regression > Linear = `lm(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df)` + `summary()`

## Reading R output

TODO: Show how to interpret R output, mapping to the SPSS output tables
participants already know. Use `broom::tidy()` for clean output.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: challenge

## Challenge 1

TODO: Run a complete analysis workflow on local data — descriptives, test,
interpret, report.

:::::::::::::::::::::::: solution

TODO: Solution code here.

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: keypoints

- Every SPSS statistical test has a direct R equivalent, usually in a single function call
- R output is more compact than SPSS — `broom::tidy()` converts it to a clean table
- The workflow in R is: load data, run test, extract results, visualize — all in a script

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
